description:
## Parent

Parent epic: `bsm-dbh` — Filtering and search issue list views.

## Background

Beadsmith must remain a desktop client for Beadwork rather than a second issue tracker. The Issue Explorer currently loads a single issue collection through the existing `list_issues` path. The filtering/search epic requires the explorer to preload the three authoritative base collections that define the feature: All Issues, Ready, and Blocked.

All Issues is every Beadwork Issue in the Current Workspace across stored statuses and must come from `bw list --all --json`; `bw list --json` is not enough because it follows Beadwork's default actionable/open semantics. Ready and Blocked are workflow views, not fields that Beadsmith should derive locally. ADR-0005 says Beadsmith must call `bw ready --json` and `bw blocked --json` and preserve those commands' membership and ordering.

This task establishes the native/RPC/frontend data-loading contract that every later sidebar, search, empty-state, and e2e slice depends on.

## What to build

Build one combined Issue Explorer load path that returns the Current Workspace path plus three normalized Issue collections:

- `allIssues`, loaded from `bw list --all --json`.
- `readyIssues`, loaded from `bw ready --json`.
- `blockedIssues`, loaded from `bw blocked --json`.

The combined load should be all-or-nothing. If any required command fails, the Issue Explorer load fails as a whole using the existing issue-list error taxonomy instead of rendering partial data or inventing local fallback semantics.

## Technical Approach

Extend the Rust Beadwork issue adapter so the existing raw Issue parser can be reused for all three commands. Keep Beadwork CLI JSON as the only integration surface. Do not parse rendered TTY text or inspect Beadwork's git storage.

Preserve the existing normalized frontend `Issue` shape for each collection. `bw blocked --json` can include extra fields such as `open_blockers`; ignore those extras for this slice and keep dependency UI sourced from the normal Issue fields. If comments/docs currently say the raw parser only represents `bw list --all --json`, update them so it is clear the parser now supports Beadwork Issue JSON returned by list/ready/blocked commands.

Expose a new combined TauRPC command, `load_issue_explorer_data`, returning the workspace path and the three collections. Keep the existing `list_issues` command available unless a separate cleanup explicitly removes it and updates all tests/docs. Update generated TypeScript bindings and the frontend Effect service to consume the combined command for the Issue Explorer. Name the new frontend payload/state around Issue Explorer data rather than a generic issue list so future readers can tell this load includes multiple Beadwork-authored views, not only one array.

The frontend success state should hold all three collections even when any or all are empty. Retire the legacy idea that an empty issue array requires a separate load status for this path; true-empty UI decisions should later derive from `allIssues.length === 0`. If this slice temporarily leaves existing empty copy in place until `bsm-dbh.8`, keep the data contract correct and add comments/tests that make the transition explicit so later agents do not reintroduce a separate service-level empty state.

## Considerations

- Ready and Blocked must not be derived from `blockedBy`, status, parent, deferral, or other fields in React or Rust.
- Preserve output order from each Beadwork command. Do not sort or join by All Issues order.
- Treat `null`, empty stdout, and `[]` as successful empty command results where the existing parser already does so.
- Reuse the existing error categories: `missingBinary`, `notBeadworkWorkspace`, `commandFailed`, `parseFailed`, and `executionFailed`.
- It is acceptable for a Ready/Blocked failure to prevent All Issues from rendering in this slice; semantically wrong partial data is worse than an explicit failure.
- Prefer the simplest reliable implementation over premature concurrency. Running the three commands sequentially is acceptable if the code and logs make clear which command failed; concurrency can be optimized later without changing the contract.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The Issue Explorer load path calls `bw list --all --json`, `bw ready --json`, and `bw blocked --json` behind one combined RPC/service operation.
- [ ] The combined response includes `workspacePath`, `allIssues`, `readyIssues`, and `blockedIssues`.
- [ ] Empty collections are represented as a successful loaded result rather than a separate legacy empty load state.
- [ ] Ready and Blocked parsing is null-tolerant and normalizes into the same frontend Issue contract as All Issues.
- [ ] Extra Blocked-only fields such as `open_blockers` are accepted/ignored and are not surfaced in UI contracts for this slice.
- [ ] If any required command fails, the combined load fails as a whole with the existing issue-list error taxonomy.
- [ ] The existing `list_issues` RPC remains available unless intentionally removed by a separate cleanup.
- [ ] Logging or error context is specific enough for a developer to tell whether All, Ready, or Blocked failed during the combined load.

## Test Plan

- Rust adapter tests for the exact command invocations for All, Ready, and Blocked.
- Rust adapter/parser tests for `null`, `[]`, representative non-empty Ready output, and representative non-empty Blocked output containing `open_blockers`.
- RPC tests for successful combined payload mapping and for whole-load failure when any one underlying command fails.
- Type generation test asserting the new `load_issue_explorer_data` binding and combined response fields exist.
- Frontend Effect service tests for loading, success-with-empty-collections, and failure mapping.

comments.text:
# Implementation Plan

## Goal

Load Issue Explorer data through one authoritative TauRPC/Effect path that returns Current Workspace plus All, Ready, and Blocked Beadwork Issue collections, preserving Beadwork CLI semantics and failing all-or-nothing.

## Tasks

1. **Generalize the Rust Beadwork issue adapter across the three required CLI views**
   - File: `src-tauri/src/issues/adapter.rs`
   - Changes: Replace the single `BW_ARGS` constant with explicit arguments for All (`["list", "--all", "--json"]`), Ready (`["ready", "--json"]`), and Blocked (`["blocked", "--json"]`). Introduce a shared helper such as `load_issues_for_view(runner, view_label, args)` that logs the view label and exact command, invokes `bw`, reuses `interpret_output`/`parse_issues`, and preserves output order. Keep `list_all_issues` as a public wrapper and add public `list_ready_issues` and `list_blocked_issues` wrappers.
   - Acceptance: Adapter tests prove the exact commands are invoked for All, Ready, and Blocked; parser behavior for `null`, empty stdout, and `[]` remains successful empty results.

2. **Export the new adapter functions and update parser documentation**
   - File: `src-tauri/src/issues/mod.rs`
   - Changes: Re-export `list_ready_issues` and `list_blocked_issues` alongside `list_all_issues`.
   - Acceptance: `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs` can call all three functions through `crate::issues`.
   - File: `src-tauri/src/issues/raw.rs`
   - Changes: Update comments that currently describe only `bw list --all --json` so they state the raw parser accepts Beadwork Issue JSON from `list`, `ready`, and `blocked`; note that unknown fields such as `open_blockers` are intentionally ignored.
   - Acceptance: Future readers do not infer a separate raw type is needed for `bw blocked --json`.

3. **Add Rust adapter tests for Ready and Blocked-specific shapes**
   - File: `src-tauri/src/issues/adapter.rs`
   - Changes: Extend the existing FakeRunner tests to cover `list_ready_issues`, `list_blocked_issues`, a representative Ready payload, and a Blocked payload containing an extra `open_blockers` field. Keep existing All tests intact.
   - Acceptance: `cd src-tauri && cargo test issues::adapter` passes; the Blocked fixture parses into the same normalized `Issue` shape and does not expose `open_blockers`.

4. **Add the combined TauRPC response and resolver while preserving `list_issues`**
   - File: `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs`
   - Changes: Add `async fn load_issue_explorer_data() -> Result<LoadIssueExplorerDataResponse, IssueListError>` to `BeadsmithApi`. Add `LoadIssueExplorerDataResponse { workspace_path, all_issues, ready_issues, blocked_issues }` as a TauRPC IPC type. Implement a helper that computes `workspace_path` once, then sequentially runs All → Ready → Blocked using the adapter functions and maps each collection into frontend `Issue` values. Leave `list_issues` and `ListIssuesResponse` available unchanged.
   - Acceptance: Generated bindings include both `list_issues` and `load_issue_explorer_data`; the new response fields generate as `workspacePath`, `allIssues`, `readyIssues`, and `blockedIssues`.

5. **Make combined-load failures all-or-nothing with view-specific context**
   - File: `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs`
   - Changes: Reuse the existing `IssueListErrorKind` taxonomy, but map errors in the combined helper with message context identifying the failed view/command, for example “Could not load Ready Issues: …”. Do not return partial data if Ready or Blocked fails after All succeeds.
   - Acceptance: Unit tests simulate All, Ready, and Blocked failures separately; each returns the existing error kind and a non-empty message that identifies the failing view.

6. **Update TauRPC binding generation tests and regenerate bindings**
   - File: `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs`
   - Changes: Extend `generates_typescript_bindings` assertions to require `LoadIssueExplorerDataResponse`, the four combined response fields, and `load_issue_explorer_data`, while keeping existing assertions for `Issue`, `IssueComment`, `IssueListErrorKind`, and `list_issues`.
   - File: `src/rpc/bindings.ts`
   - Changes: Regenerate via the existing Specta/TauRPC path; do not hand-edit except as generated output.
   - Acceptance: `cd src-tauri && cargo test generates_typescript_bindings` passes and the generated `Router` exposes both RPC methods.

7. **Replace the frontend issue-list loader contract with Issue Explorer data loading**
   - File: `src/issues/issue-loader.ts`
   - Changes: Import the generated combined response type. Rename or add types around Issue Explorer data, e.g. `IssueExplorerData`, `IssueExplorerLoadState`, and `ISSUE_EXPLORER_LOADING_STATE`. The success state should include `workspacePath`, `allIssues`, `readyIssues`, and `blockedIssues` even when arrays are empty. Remove the service-level `empty` state from the new load path. Change `IssueTransportClient` to expose `loadIssueExplorerData()`, and have `createTauRpcIssueTransport()` call `rpc.load_issue_explorer_data()`.
   - Acceptance: Empty arrays from the RPC resolve to `{ status: "success", allIssues: [], readyIssues: [], blockedIssues: [], workspacePath }`, not a separate `empty` state.

8. **Rewrite frontend loader tests for the combined contract**
   - File: `src/issues/issue-loader.test.ts`
   - Changes: Update test helpers and transport fakes to use `loadIssueExplorerData`. Assert loading, non-empty success, empty-array success, and failure mapping. Add an assertion that Ready/Blocked arrays are preserved exactly and not derived from All.
   - Acceptance: `pnpm test -- src/issues/issue-loader.test.ts` passes.

9. **Wire App and Issue Explorer to the new state shape while keeping current UI behavior on All Issues**
   - File: `src/App.tsx`
   - Changes: Import the renamed loading constant and TauRPC loader. Update `workspaceTextFor` to handle loading/success/failure only. Keep sidebar view buttons disabled unless another bead enables switching.
   - Acceptance: The sidebar Current Workspace still shows the successful `workspacePath` and “Unavailable” on failure.
   - File: `src/issues/IssueExplorer.tsx`
   - Changes: Update props and internal reads from `issues` to `allIssues` for the currently rendered list/detail. Keep loading/failure UI. Render the existing empty-state copy when `state.status === "success" && state.allIssues.length === 0`; this is UI derivation, not a service-level load state. Add a short comment if useful that Ready/Blocked are preloaded for later view-selection beads.
   - Acceptance: Existing row selection and detail rendering behavior remains unchanged for All Issues; a successful empty All collection still renders “No issues found”.

10. **Update React component tests for the new successful-empty shape**
    - File: `src/issues/IssueExplorer.test.tsx`
    - Changes: Update `successState`/fixture helpers to build the new success state with `allIssues`, `readyIssues`, and `blockedIssues`. Add or adjust a test proving `allIssues: []` renders the empty UI without requiring `status: "empty"`.
    - Acceptance: `pnpm test -- src/issues/IssueExplorer.test.tsx` passes.

11. **Update e2e direct RPC coverage to exercise the new combined command**
    - File: `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.success.spec.ts`
    - Changes: Change the direct native RPC smoke test from `TauRPC__list_issues` to `TauRPC__load_issue_explorer_data` and assert the fixture title appears in `allIssues`. If fixture semantics are stable, also assert `readyIssues` and/or `blockedIssues` are arrays without deriving membership locally. Leave UI row/detail assertions intact because the app should now load through the combined path.
    - Acceptance: `pnpm test:e2e:issue-list` reaches the new command and the UI still renders real Beadwork data.

12. **Run validation and formatting**
    - File: n/a
    - Changes: Run targeted tests first, then broader checks: `cd src-tauri && cargo test`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm check`, and, where `bw` plus the desktop test stack are available, `pnpm test:e2e:issue-list`.
    - Acceptance: All mandatory unit tests and lint/check commands pass; any skipped e2e run is recorded with the environment reason.

## Files to Modify

- `src-tauri/src/issues/adapter.rs` - generalized command helper, Ready/Blocked public wrappers, logs, and adapter tests.
- `src-tauri/src/issues/mod.rs` - re-export new adapter functions.
- `src-tauri/src/issues/raw.rs` - documentation update for shared parser and ignored `open_blockers`.
- `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs` - new `load_issue_explorer_data` TauRPC method, combined response type, all-or-nothing resolver, contextual error mapping, and binding assertions.
- `src/rpc/bindings.ts` - regenerated TauRPC/Specta bindings.
- `src/issues/issue-loader.ts` - frontend Effect service and state contract for Issue Explorer data.
- `src/issues/issue-loader.test.ts` - combined loader unit tests.
- `src/App.tsx` - call the combined loader and update workspace state handling.
- `src/issues/IssueExplorer.tsx` - consume `allIssues` from the combined success state and derive empty UI from `allIssues.length`.
- `src/issues/IssueExplorer.test.tsx` - update fixtures/helpers and add successful-empty UI coverage.
- `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.success.spec.ts` - native RPC smoke coverage for `load_issue_explorer_data`.

## New Files

- None expected.

## Dependencies

- Tasks 1-3 must land before Tasks 4-6 because the RPC resolver depends on the new adapter functions.
- Tasks 4-6 must land before Tasks 7-11 because frontend TypeScript types come from regenerated `src/rpc/bindings.ts`.
- Tasks 7-10 should be implemented together because `IssueExplorerLoadState` shape changes affect both `App.tsx` and `IssueExplorer.tsx` tests.
- Task 11 depends on the app already using the combined loader and the new TauRPC command being generated.
- Task 12 depends on all implementation tasks.

## Risks

- The issue does not explicitly mandate command sequencing. Use All → Ready → Blocked unless a reviewer requests another order; preserve each command’s own order.
- Timeout/cancellation behavior for a hanging `bw` command is not specified and should remain out of scope unless the supervisor expands requirements.
- `bw blocked --json` currently parses because `RawIssue` does not deny unknown fields. Future parser hardening could break `open_blockers` tolerance unless covered by the new test.
- Existing `list_issues` e2e/direct callers must keep working; do not remove the method in this slice.
- UI view switching/search are downstream concerns. This slice should preload `readyIssues`/`blockedIssues` but continue rendering All Issues until later beads enable view selection.
- I could not add this plan as a Beadwork comment from this subagent because only file read/write tools are available, not `bw`/shell execution. The plan is written to the required artifact path.

}

title: Load Issue Explorer data through one authoritative All/Ready/Blocked RPC
